text: Welcome to This is My Architecture. I'm Todd and today we're in New York City. Here with me I'm
joined with Mike from Synchronet. Mike, welcome. Thank you. Can you tell us who Synchronet is?
Synchronet is an advanced consulting partner. We've developed a platform that helps with the
provisioning and automation of Amazon Workspaces. Great. Let's jump right into the
architecture and see the starting point of what makes this all work. Sure. So we have a
console where administrators can come to set up the business logic that runs the
automation of the WorkSpaces provisioning as well as end users are able to come
to that console as well to do some self-service functions on their
workspaces. Okay great. So the end users being the customers looks like they're
leveraging Amazon Cognito to log in securely. Exactly. So we didn't want to be in the
business of storing credentials for customer users so we put in Cognito.
That lets us set up a SAML authentication flow with the customer's identity provider.
Okay great. So third-party identity provider logging into the console is their
kind of user experience. Talk to us a little bit about what happens next.
Sure. So we work with the directory services for the customer. We give them a
cloud formation template that puts this Lambda into their environment that's able
to communicate with through API Gateway to provide us with all of the users and
groups that are in their organization. Okay great. So from a customer
perspective the resource that they have is a Lambda function that they're hosting.
API Gateway is backed by a Lambda function and it looks like some information might be
passed to a data store. Right exactly. So as you said the API Gateway is backed by
this Lambda that handles all of the CRUD operations for the database. Okay great.
So from that perspective the request right is always about how do I automate the
provisioning of workspaces? How do we make this happen in the customer's account?
Exactly. So this Lambda also does the automation so every time it gets a new batch of users and groups
it will do a calculation to find out do I have new users, do I have users that need
to be changed and once it finds those situations it puts them into an SQS queue.
Once those jobs are queued up does it act as a trigger? What's the next step to the flow?
Exactly. So now that SQS can trigger Lambdas directly it triggers these Lambda functions that
really do the work of loading up the step functions that do the work of provisioning,
modifying, stopping and terminating the workspaces. So step functions used to
coordinate the other Lambda functions that are running in the environment which
contain their own business logic. Talk to us about the architectural decision that went
into leveraging step functions. Sure. So there are a number of timing things
that have to happen with this. For example workspaces take about 20 minutes to spin up.
So we want to send out an invitation to the end user saying that the workspace is ready.
But we don't want to do that until we know that it's up and healthy and running.
So the step function waits inside of it, makes sure that it's running and then sends the invitation.
Ah, very clever. So leveraging the wait state feature within step functions.
Exactly.
Make sure you're really not burning unnecessary execution time in Lambda.
That's right.
Very, very cool. So at this point talk to us a little bit about the scale and what would this provide me as a customer
who's looking to automate X number of workspaces.
Right. So we work with enterprise customers. So this is at the scale of thousands of workspaces.
So at that scale you run into rate limits and those kinds of things.
And that's kind of why we designed it and we've tested it at a thousand workspaces.
Okay, great. So you guys are handling all of the work that the customers would have to learn about
and work through the service limits increases, etc. as your platform.
So that's what's provided to the customer.
Exactly.
Great. Well, thank you. This is a very interesting use case and I'd like to thank you for joining today.
Thank you for having me.
And thank you for watching This Is My Architecture.
